step_by_step_reasoning: We first identify the user (UserCompanyDeveloper) triggering a workflow in the ITSM tool (ThirdParty). This ITSM tool calls AWS Control Tower to provision new accounts. Next, Service Catalog deploys a tagged VPC into the vended account. An automated orchestrator reads these tags to establish a Transit Gateway attachment between the vended TGW and the core network TGW.

graph.name: DISH Network Automated Self-Service Account and Network Provisioning
graph.notes: Automates account and VPC provisioning for hundreds of accounts within six months using Control Tower, Service Catalog, and Transit Gateway.

nodes.name: Internal User
nodes.notes: Internal stakeholders who trigger automated self-service AWS account creation.

nodes.name: ITSM Tool
nodes.notes: Triggers the account baseline and provisioning workflows upon approval.

nodes.name: Control Tower
nodes.notes: Launches the account factory to provision a fresh, baselined AWS account.

nodes.name: Service Catalog
nodes.notes: Provisions a tagged VPC and standard resources in the newly vended account.

nodes.name: Vended VPC
nodes.notes: VPC deployed in the vended account containing tag metadata for network configuration.

nodes.name: Vended Transit Gateway
nodes.notes: Local TGW attachment automatically configured using tag metadata.

nodes.name: Core Transit Gateway
nodes.notes: Central hub TGW residing in the Network account to connect the entire estate.

edges.notes: Kicks off account factory baseline creation.
Deploys VPC with tags into vended account.
Creates TGW attachment from vended VPC to core central network.
